title: Leucanthemum glaucophyllum
taxonomy: Leucanthemum glaucophyllum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of daisy native to the Mediterranean region. The plant belongs to the genus Leucanthemum, which includes several species of daisies. Leucanthemum glaucophyllum is closely related to other species within the same genus and family. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure.
uses: Leucanthemum glaucophyllum, also known as the white-flowered Oxeye Daisy, has been utilized in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which could be beneficial in treating minor wounds and skin irritations. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Leucanthemum glaucophyllum for medicinal purposes. Traditional folk remedies suggest that the plant can also be used to treat gastrointestinal issues and reduce fever. The plant's potential uses and benefits are still being studied, and more information is required to fully understand its medicinal applications.
benefits: Leucanthemum glaucophyllum, also known as the Glaucous Oxeye Daisy, is a lesser-known species of the Oxeye Daisy family. However, research on its close relatives suggests that Leucanthemum species may have potential health benefits due to their high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help protect against cell damage. Additionally, some studies on other daisy species have found that they may have antimicrobial and antiviral properties. More research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Leucanthemum glaucophyllum specifically.

intro: Leucanthemum glaucophyllum, also known as White Edged Oxeye Daisy, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum glaucophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Leucanthemum glaucophyllum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and topical creams, as well as being incorporated into herbal blends and supplements to leverage its medicinal benefits.
